payload.author: claude
payload.body:
VALIDATED via SCP-121 (GO). Thesis sharpened — the epic's framing shifts from 'offline-first relay (merge math is the moat)' to:

AGENT-OPERATED MULTIPLAYER KANBAN. The product is the board that LLM agent fleets natively drive (CLI + --json + MCP — already built), made multiplayer by an auth-gated realtime relay. Core value: parallel agents (and their humans) see each other's claims INSTANTLY so they don't collide — collisions cost real tokens + rework. Merge/de-collision is the durable floor, not the pitch.

Confirmed constraints from validation:
- Offline-first IS required for v1 (full Path A, not a reduced online-only relay).
- Identity: principal = human account; agent acts AS the user + stamps acting model; attribution = '{model} on behalf of {user}'.
- Buyer: small/remote dev teams running agents. Business: SaaS, not a resold backend. Willingness confirmed; pricing unit TBD.

Phase plan unchanged in spirit (1: single-tenant cloud hub + real login; 2: multi-tenant storage + external pub/sub; 3: offline sync-cursor protocol; 4: billing/quotas/teams) but every spike is now re-pointed at the agent-collision use case.
